channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Top Fashion Brands Have a Supply Chain Battle Against Extreme Heat
item.description: The global garment industry is beginning to address impacts of record temperatures, which threaten tens of millions of workers and erode productivity.
item.dc:creator: Azman Usmani

item.title: UK Issues Electricity Supply Warning as Heat Wave Strains Grid
item.description: The UK grid operator issued a rare summer warning, citing tight electricity supplies as a blistering heat wave strains energy systems across Europe.
item.dc:creator: Eamon Farhat

item.title: UK, EDF Reach Deal to Extend Life of Sizewell B Nuclear Plant
item.description: The UK government reached a draft deal with Electricite de France SA to extend the life of a key nuclear plant beyond the middle of the century, as the nation seeks to cut reliance on fossil fuels for power generation.
item.dc:creator: Jess Shankleman

item.title: Meat Giant JBS Pulls Back From 2040 Net-Zero Emissions Goal
item.description: JBS NV, the world’s largest meatpacker, is stepping back from a 2040 net-zero emissions goal, dropping targets for the indirect emissions that make up the bulk of its environmental footprint.
item.dc:creator: Ilena Peng

item.title: California Chosen for Peak Energy’s Sodium-Ion Battery Plant
item.description: Peak Energy said it’s building what it describes as the first commercial US manufacturing facility for grid-scale sodium-ion energy storage systems in Sacramento, California.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Ma

item.title: How Climate Change Could Raise Your Water Bill
item.description: Extreme weather is already fueling higher insurance premiums and grocery prices. A new study based on Santa Cruz, California suggests water rates are also at risk.
item.dc:creator: Emma Court

item.title: London Moves to Expand Influence in $140 Billion ILS Market
item.description: The world’s biggest hub for commercial and specialty risk is angling for a much more prominent role in the $140 billion global market for insurance-linked securities.
item.dc:creator: Gautam Naik

item.title: EU Plans More Flexibility for Industry in Carbon Overhaul
item.description: The European Union aims to give its ailing industrial sector more flexibility in an upcoming reform of the bloc’s flagship carbon market, while ensuring companies boost investment in clean technologies.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Krukowska

item.title: Private Equity Funds Seek Climate Experts as Risks Rise
item.description: Investors are turning to specialist tools and companies to understand escalating threats from extreme weather events.
item.dc:creator: Ishika Mookerjee, Mary Hui, Cao Ban

item.title: Heat Wave Threatens Output at Five French Nuclear Reactors
item.description: France’s latest heat wave is straining the country’s power system, with Electricite de France SA warning that as many as five nuclear plants could face output curbs in the coming days as soaring temperatures affect rivers used for cooling.
item.dc:creator: Will Mathis, Joe Wertz
